textByHash.94bc46b86d1ad38427ce39c11bf1874279a79b9a: Oooh, my dear, have you heard the news?
textByHash.1ddd6c3e16881f1c43704a296a1660f5676fa5b5: It's terrible, absolutely terrible! Those poor people!
textByHash.5a4b5fd6bbf43bab716a6f584bef9d6a1937b794: And who'd have ever thought such a sweet old gentleman would do such a thing?
textByHash.272053553d2cb6b1cecb72eaf02c723eb08f01c2: Oh yes, my dear. It was terrible! TERRIBLE!
textByHash.824c46567a6abdc500a7bc9ce242d1dd1070df97: But tell me - have you been around here before, or are you new to these parts?
textByHash.278817f291a0055469ab31a043686aaf48ead32a: Aah, perhaps you missed the excitement. It's that old man in this house here. Do you know him?
textByHash.975d920f8ae7a9f40f4da994b061886040b77237: When he first moved here, he didn't bring much. From the window you could see he just had some old furniture and a few dusty ornaments.
textByHash.7b2537044e2caaf8ed782985391f8ad7c394f6f3: Also he always seemed so poor. When I went round to collect donations for the Draynor Manor Restoration Fund, he couldn't spare them a penny!
textByHash.675594e68ca21ef863425b21b36be3726426a67e: Well, just you look in there now!
textByHash.d6f790c25b90867b7f2f545d6a9da8517f8487cd: You see? It's full of jewellery and decorations! And all those expensive things appeared just after the bank got robbed.
textByHash.c690655e8bf876683bd58e96ea9f523d1d42663d: He changed his hat too - he used to wear a scruffy old black thing, but suddenly he was wearing that party hat!
textByHash.6208b6399778e3cae3362d48df9a2b305a47fe69: Oooh, my dear, I'm SURE of it! I went upstairs in his house once, while he was out walking, and do you know what I found?
textByHash.1534b6254642bca7886756a28495b8259e69d069: No, it was a telescope! It was pointing right at the bank! He was spying on the bankers, planning the big robbery!
textByHash.ed15a6d03ca72ef57beb1bddb713cf8d6a5b38c8: I bet if you go and look through it now, you'll find it's pointing somewhere different now he's finished with the bank.
textByHash.2756ec091d1249bc50c7275208241872804b53da: Oh, really? Well, do keep an eye on him - I just KNOW he's planning something...
textByHash.b97b506a3d2510240bb1d2385c50298721ef0953: Ah, so you'd have seen the changes here. It's that old man in this house here. Do you know him?
textByHash.48a3bce00294481c0a48beddea91e2097e180402: Oooh, my dear, had you not heard?
textByHash.521d1bc78c01a4470ac136cce20fc632af250f0c: Oh, you must quickly go and speak to the bank guard outside the bank. He'll tell you all about it, oooh, such a shock it was...
textByHash.4036b84c7516a3ed8b6e61c11a0827cc3f6ef4e0: I, my dear, am a concerned citizen of Draynor Village. Ever since the Council allowed those farmers to set up their stalls here, we've had a constant flow of thieves and murderers through our fair village, and I decided
textByHash.9c26a9ab933fe77f725743933428998463031249: that someone HAD to stand up and keep an eye on the situation.
textByHash.257b81afb3d285d96a80e2adadf6f0fae9d8fdfc: I also do voluntary work for the Draynor Manor Restoration Fund. We're campaigning to have Draynor Manor turned into a museum before the wet-rot destroys it completely.
textByHash.80f6443144a946bca56e405c5336150abd1dc2ff: Oooh! I have new information to share with you.
